question.text: What is the ARN (AMFI Registration Number)?

answer.text: A number assigned to every mutual fund scheme
A unique registration number issued by AMFI to qualified mutual fund distributors
An account number assigned to mutual fund investors
A number assigned by SEBI to each AMC

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ARN (AMFI Registration Number) is a unique identification number issued by AMFI to individuals and organizations who have qualified to distribute mutual fund products. To obtain an ARN, one must pass the NISM Series V-A or V-B certification exam and register with AMFI. The ARN must be quoted in all mutual fund transaction documents. **The Trap:** ARN is for distributors, not for investors or schemes. An investor should verify their distributor's ARN to ensure they are dealing with a registered intermediary. ARN must be renewed periodically. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always share your ARN with retired investors. It builds trust and shows you are a registered, qualified professional. Investors can verify your ARN on the AMFI website. Transparency about your registration status is part of ethical practice.
